heading: STEM education
label: 1862i STEM education
notes: References in TextSections 1862h to 1862j of this title, referred to in subsecs. (a) and (i), was in the original “this Act”, meaning , , , known as the Scientific and Advanced-Technology Act of 1992, which enacted this section and sections 1862h and 1862j of this title and amended . For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title of 1992 Amendment note set out under and Tables.Pub. CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Scientific and Advanced-Technology Act of 1992, and not as part of the National Science Foundation Act of 1950 which comprises this chapter.

Amendments2022—, substituted “STEM education” for “Scientific and technical education” in section catchline.Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(A)Subsec. (a). , in introductory provisions, inserted “and education to prepare the skilled technical workforce to meet workforce demands” before “, and to improve” and substituted “core education courses in STEM fields” for “core education courses in science and mathematics” and “(including veterans and individuals engaged in work in the home) and on building a pathway from secondary schools to associate-degree-granting institutions, to careers that require technical training, and shall be designed” for “(including work in the home), and shall be designed”.Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(B)(ii), substituted “STEM education” for “scientific and technical education” in heading.Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(B)(i)Subsec. (a)(1). , inserted “and study” after “development” and substituted “core STEM courses” for “core science and mathematics courses”.Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(B)(iii)Subsec. (a)(2). , substituted “STEM and advanced- technology fields” for “science, mathematics, and advanced-technology fields”.Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(B)(iv)Subsec. (a)(3)(A). , inserted “to support the advanced- technology industries that drive the competitiveness of the United States in the global economy” before semicolon at end.Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(B)(v)Subsec. (a)(4). , which directed substitution of “STEM and advanced-technology fields” for “scientific and advanced- technology fields”, was executed by making the substitution for “scientific and advanced-technology fields” to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(B)(vi)Subsec. (a)(5). , substituted “advanced STEM and advanced- technology” for “advanced scientific and technical education”.Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(B)(vii)Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Director shall award grants for the establishment of centers of excellence, not to exceed 12 in number, among associate-degree-granting colleges. Centers shall meet one or both of the following criteria:Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(b)“(1) Exceptional instructional programs in advanced-technology fields.“(2) Excellence in undergraduate education in mathematics and science.The centers shall serve as national and regional clearinghouses and models for the benefit of both colleges and secondary schools, and shall provide seminars and programs to disseminate model curricula and model teaching methods and instructional materials to other associate-degree-granting colleges in the geographic region served by the center.”Subsec. (c)(1)(A). , substituted “to encourage the development of career and educational pathways with multiple entry and exit points leading to credentials and degrees, and to assist students pursuing pathways in STEM fields to transition from associate-degree-granting colleges to bachelor- degree-granting institutions, through such means as—” for “to encourage students to pursue bachelor degrees in mathematics, science, engineering, or technology, and to assist students pursuing bachelor degrees in mathematics, science, engineering, or technology to make the transition from associate-degree-granting colleges to bachelor-degree-granting institutions, through such means as—” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(C)(i)(I)(aa)Subsec. (c)(1)(A)(i). , substituted “to develop articulation agreements that ensure” for “to ensure”.Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(C)(i)(I)(bb)Subsec. (c)(1)(A)(ii). , substituted “the career and educational pathways supported by the articulation agreements” for “courses at the bachelor-degree-granting institution”.Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(C)(i)(I)(cc)Subsec. (c)(1)(B). , struck out concluding provisions which read as follows: “Funds used by eligible partnerships to carry out clauses (i) and (ii) shall be from non-Federal sources. In-cash and in-kind resources used by eligible partnerships to carry out clauses (i) and (ii) shall not be considered to be contributions for purposes of applying subsection (i)(3).”Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(C)(i)(II)(cc)Subsec. (c)(1)(B)(i). , inserted “veterans and individuals engaged in” before “work in the home”.Pub. L. 117–167, § 10312(f)(2)(C)(i)(II)(aa)Subsec. (c)(1)(B)(iii). , substituted “institutions or work sites” for “bachelor’s-degree-granting institutions” and inserted “or industry internships” after “summer programs”.Pub. Effective Date of 1998 AmendmentAmendment by effective , except as otherwise provided in , see , set out as a note under , Education.Pub. L. 105–244Pub. L. 105–244section 3 of Pub. L. 105–244section 1001 of Title 20Oct. 1, 1998

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–402, § 2132 Stat. 5343Dec. 31, 2018“Congress finds the following:“(1)To remain competitive in the global economy, foster greater innovation, and provide a foundation for shared prosperity, the United States needs a workforce with the right mix of skills to meet the diverse needs of the economy.“(2)Evidence indicates that the returns on investments in technical skills in the labor market are strong when students successfully complete their education and gain credentials sought by employers.“(3)The responsibility for developing and sustaining a skilled technical workforce is fragmented across many groups, including educators, students, workers, employers, Federal, State, and local governments, civic associations, and other stakeholders. Such groups need to be able to coordinate and cooperate successfully with each other.“(4)Coordination among students, community colleges, secondary and post-secondary institutions, and employers would improve educational outcomes.“(5)Promising experiments currently underway may guide innovation and reform, but scalability of some of those experiments has not yet been tested.“(6)Evidence suggests that integration of academic education, technical skills development, and hands-on work experience improves outcomes and return on investment for students in secondary and post-secondary education and for skilled technical workers in different career stages.“(7)Outcomes show that mentoring can increase STEM student engagement and the rate of completion of STEM post-secondary degrees.”

children.heading: National advanced STEM education program
children.label: a National advanced STEM education program

children.text: the development and study of model instructional programs in advanced-technology fields and in core STEM courses;

the professional development of faculty and instructors, both full- and part-time, who provide instruction in STEM and advanced- technology fields;1So in original. The space between “advanced-” and “technology” probably should not appear.1

children.label: A
children.text: involve associate-degree-granting colleges and other appropriate public and private sector entities to support the advanced- technology industries that drive the competitiveness of the United States in the global economy;1

children.label: B
children.text: provide for private sector donations, faculty opportunities to have short-term assignments with industry, sharing of program costs, equipment loans, and the cooperative use of laboratories, plants, and other facilities, and provision for state-of-the-art work experience opportunities for students enrolled in such programs; and

children.label: C
children.text: encourage participation of individuals identified in section 1885a or 1885b of this title;
the acquisition of state-of-the-art instrumentation essential to programs designed to prepare and upgrade students in STEM and advanced-technology fields; and

the development and dissemination of instructional materials in support of improving the advanced STEM and advanced- technology and training capabilities of associate-degree-granting colleges, including programs for students who are not pursuing a science degree.1

children.heading: Centers of scientific and technical education
children.label: b Centers of scientific and technical education
A
children.text: Exceptional instructional programs in advanced-technology fields.

children.label: B
children.text: Excellence in undergraduate STEM education.

children.heading: Purposes
children.label: 2 Purposes
children.text: The centers shall serve as national and regional clearinghouses and models for the benefit of both colleges and secondary schools, and shall provide seminars and programs to disseminate model curricula and model teaching methods and instructional materials to other associate-degree-granting colleges.

children.label: A
children.text: coordinate research, training, and education activities funded by awards under subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: share information and best practices; or

children.label: C
children.text: promote collaboration between academic institutions, workforce development programs, labor organizations, and industry to communicate and meet workforce education and training needs.

children.label: i
children.text: examining curricula to develop articulation agreements that ensure that academic credit earned at the associate-degree-granting college is transferable to bachelor-degree-granting institutions;

children.label: ii
children.text: informing teachers from the associate-degree-granting college on the specific requirements of the career and educational pathways supported by the articulation agreements; and

children.label: iii
children.text: providing summer educational programs for students from the associate-degree-granting college to encourage such students’ subsequent matriculation at bachelor-degree-granting institutions.

children.label: i
children.text: counsel students, including students who have been in the workforce (including veterans and individuals engaged in work in the home), about the requirements and course offerings of the bachelor-degree-granting institution;

children.label: ii
children.text: conduct workshops and orientation sessions to ensure that students are familiar with programs, including laboratories and financial aid programs, at the bachelor-degree-granting institution;

children.label: iii
children.text: provide students with research experiences at institutions or work sites participating in the partnership, including stipend support for students participating in summer programs or industry internships; and

children.label: iv
children.text: provide faculty mentors for students participating in activities under clause (iii), including summer salary support for faculty mentors.

children.heading: Outreach grants
children.label: 2 Outreach grants
children.text: The Director shall make grants to associate-degree-granting colleges with outstanding STEM programs to strengthen relationships with secondary schools and, as appropriate, elementary schools, in the community served by the college by improving STEM education and encouraging the interest and aptitude of students at these schools for careers in STEM and advanced-technology fields through such means as developing articulation agreements or dual credit courses with local secondary schools, or other means as the Director determines appropriate, to enable students to satisfy entrance and course requirements at the associate-degree-granting college.

children.heading: Mentor training grants
children.label: 3 Mentor training grants
children.text: The Director shall establish a program to encourage and make grants available to institutions of higher education that award associate degrees to recruit and train individuals from STEM fields to mentor students who are described in section 1885a or 1885b of this title in order to assist those students in identifying, qualifying for, and entering higher-paying technical jobs in those fields, including jobs at Federal and academic laboratories.

children.heading: Grants for associate degree programs in STEM fields
children.label: d Grants for associate degree programs in STEM fields

children.heading: In-demand workforce grants
children.label: 1 In-demand workforce grants
children.text: The Director shall award grants to junior or community colleges to develop or improve associate degree or certificate programs in STEM fields, with respect to the region in which the respective college is located, and an in-demand industry sector or occupation.

children.label: A
children.text: applications that consist of a partnership between the applying junior or community college and individual employers or an employer consortia, or industry or sector partnerships, and may include a university or other organization with demonstrated expertise in academic program development;3So in original.3

children.label: B
children.text: applications that demonstrate current and future workforce demand in occupations directly related to the proposed associate degree or certificate program;

children.label: C
children.text: applications that include commitments by the partnering employers or employer consortia, or industry or sector partnerships, to offer apprenticeships, internships, or other applied learning opportunities to students enrolled in the proposed associate degree or certificate program;

children.label: D
children.text: applications that include outreach plans and goals for recruiting and enrolling women and other underrepresented populations in STEM fields in the proposed associate degree or certificate program;

children.label: E
children.text: applications that describe how the applying junior or community college will support the collection of information and data for purposes of evaluation of the proposed associate degree or certificate program; and

children.label: F
children.text: as appropriate, applications that apply the best practices for STEM education and technical skills education through distance learning or in a simulated work environment, as determined by research described in subsection (f); and

children.label: G
children.text: applications that incorporate distance learning tools and approaches.

children.heading: Grants for STEM degree applied learning opportunities
children.label: e Grants for STEM degree applied learning opportunities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall award grants to institutions of higher education partnering with private sector employers or private sector employer consortia, or industry or sector partnerships, that commit to offering apprenticeships, internships, research opportunities, or applied learning experiences to enrolled students in identified STEM baccalaureate degree programs.

children.label: A
children.text: to develop curricula and programs for apprenticeship, internships, research opportunities, or applied learning experiences; or

children.label: B
children.text: to provide matching funds to incentivize partnership and participation by private sector employers and industry.

children.label: i
children.text: the applying institution of higher education; and

children.label: ii
children.text: individual employers or an employer consortia, or industry or sector partnerships;3

children.label: B
children.text: applications that demonstrate current and future workforce demand in occupations directly related to the identified STEM fields;

children.label: C
children.text: applications that include outreach plans and goals for recruiting and enrolling women and other underrepresented populations in STEM fields;

children.label: D
children.text: applications that describe how the institution of higher education will support the collection and information of data for purposes of the evaluation of identified STEM degree programs; and

children.label: E
children.text: applications that incorporate distance learning tools and approaches.

children.heading: Grants for computer-based and online STEM education courses
children.label: f Grants for computer-based and online STEM education courses

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director of the National Science Foundation shall award competitive grants to institutions of higher education or nonprofit organizations to conduct research on student outcomes and determine best practices for STEM education and technical skills education through distance learning or in a simulated work environment.

children.label: A
children.text: post-secondary courses for technical skills development for STEM occupations;

children.label: B
children.text: improving high-school level career and technical education in STEM subjects;

children.label: C
children.text: encouraging and sustaining interest and achievement levels in STEM subjects among women and other populations historically underrepresented in STEM studies and careers; and

children.label: D
children.text: combining computer-based and online STEM education and skills development with traditional mentoring and other mentoring arrangements, apprenticeships, internships, and other applied learning opportunities.

children.heading: Coordination with other Federal departments
children.label: g Coordination with other Federal departments
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Director shall consult, cooperate, and coordinate, to enhance program effectiveness and to avoid duplication, with the programs and policies of other relevant Federal agencies.

children.label: A
children.text: up to $5,000,000 to carry out the activities under subsection (d) for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2026, subject to the availability of appropriations;

children.label: B
children.text: up to $2,500,000 to carry out the activities under subsection (e) for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2026, subject to the availability of appropriations; and

children.label: C
children.text: not less than $3,000,000 to carry out the activities under subsection (f) for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2026, subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: Limitation on funding
children.label: 2 Limitation on funding
children.text: Amounts made available to carry out subsections (d), (e), and (f) shall be derived from amounts appropriated or otherwise made available to the National Science Foundation.

children.heading: Limitation on funding
children.label: 3 Limitation on funding
children.text: To qualify for a grant under this section, an associate-degree-granting college, or consortium thereof, shall provide assurances adequate to the Director that it will not decrease its level of spending of funds from non-Federal sources on advanced scientific and technical education and training programs.
award grants on a competitive, merit basis;
ensure an equitable geographic distribution of grant awards;
establish and maintain a readily accessible inventory of the programs assisted under sections 1862h to 1862j of this title; and

designate an officer of the National Science Foundation to serve as a liaison with associate-degree-granting institutions for the purpose of enhancing the role of such institutions in the activities of the Foundation.

the term advanced-technology includes technological fields such as advanced manufacturing, agricultural-, biological- and chemical-technologies, energy and environmental technologies, engineering technologies, information technologies, micro and nano-technologies, cybersecurity technologies, geospatial technologies, and new, emerging technology areas;4So in original. The defined term probably should be within quotation marks.4

children.label: A
children.text: is a nonprofit institution that offers a 2-year associate-degree program or a 2-year certificate program; or

children.label: B
children.text: is a proprietary institution that offers a 2-year associate-degree program;

the term “bachelor-degree-granting institution” means an institution of higher education (as determined under section 101 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 []) that offers a baccalaureate degree program;20 U.S.C. 1001
the term “eligible partnership” means one or more associate-degree-granting colleges in partnership with one or more other entities;
the term “in-demand industry sector or occupation” has the meaning given the term in ;section 3102 of title 29
the term “junior or community college” has the meaning given the term in section 312 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ();20 U.S.C. 1058
the term “region” means a labor market area, as that term is defined in ;section 3102 of title 29
the terms “science, technology, engineering, or mathematics” or “STEM” mean science, technology, engineering, and mathematics, including computer science and cybersecurity; and

the term skilled technical workforce has the meaning given such term in section 4(b) of the Innovations in Mentoring, Training, and Apprenticeships Act ().442 U.S.C. 1862p5See References in Text note below.5
